title: Parole rate is up in Mass., including for people with life sentences
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts parole rate increased by 3.5% in 2024, partially due to the Commonwealth v. Sheldon Mattis decision.
The parole rate for life sentence hearings rose from 57% in 2023 to 72% in 2024, according to annual reports.
Over 1,400 people are on parole in Massachusetts, including 400 serving life sentences and 1,280 under continued supervision.

title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: New Hampshire officials released a previously unseen photo of Rosalie Miller to aid in solving her 1997 homicide case.
Miller, 36, was strangled; her body found in Auburn on January 20, 1997, after disappearing on December 8, 1996.
Authorities seek information from anyone who saw Miller in December 1996, especially near Londonderry Turnpike, Auburn.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, charged with murder after allegedly shooting Javon Lawson, 35, in Cranston, Rhode Island.
Sousa allegedly caused a car crash in Swansea, Massachusetts, killing two people after fleeing the Cranston shooting.
Police found Sousa with a pistol; he was arrested and hospitalized, expected to face further charges.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins linked to potential trade for St. Louis Blues center Robert Thomas, who has surpassed 80 points the last two seasons.
Bruins would need to part with several draft picks and blue-chip prospects to acquire 26-year-old Thomas from St. Louis.
St. Louis Blues not under immediate pressure to trade Thomas before the deadline, holding leverage in potential negotiations.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Maine approved a $3,000 fine for a Brooklyn couple accused of poisoning trees to improve their Rockport view.
The couple, Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett, deny guilt, despite a settlement without an admission of responsibility.
The neighbor's family urged the couple to donate to coastal preservation in response to the incident.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Wide receivers KC Concepcion (Texas A&M) and Omar Cooper (Indiana) impressed at the NFL Combine, enhancing their draft prospects.
Edge rushers Romello Height (Texas Tech), Dani Dennis-Sutton (Penn State), and Malachi Lawrence (UCF) showed strong performances as Day 2 targets.
Offensive tackle Max Iheanachor (Arizona State) stands out with elite athleticism, possibly a first-round option for Patriots at No. 31 pick.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are unlikely to trade major draft capital to acquire top players like A.J. Brown or Maxx Crosby.
The Eagles and Raiders have set high asking prices for Brown and Crosby, hindering potential trades with New England.
Coach Mike Vrabel emphasizes the strategy of building through the draft with the Patriots having 11 picks in 2026.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power approved to build Massachusetts's largest battery energy storage system at former ExxonMobil site in Everett.
The facility spans 16.5 acres and includes two substations, 816 lithium-ion battery enclosures, and underground transmission lines.
The project supports clean energy transitions and is part of Everett's wider 'Docklands' mixed-use development initiative.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Connor Storrie hosted SNL's Feb. 28 episode with musical guest Mumford & Sons, marking the show's return post-Super Bowl and Winter Olympics.
Storrie was joined by his 'Heated Rivalry' co-star Hudson Williams, who appeared in a skating sketch and introduced the musical guest.
US Olympic hockey team members, including Hilary Knight, appeared during SNL's monologue, humorously addressing recent controversies with President Trump.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s loss against the Red Bulls
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 1-0 to the New York Red Bulls at Sports Illustrated Stadium on Saturday afternoon.
Revolution struggled against Red Bulls' pressing, managing only 27% possession in the first half.
A missed opportunity in the 68th minute kept New England from equalizing, leading to their second straight defeat.
